Scenario.title: RedDelta Modified PlugX Infection Chain: Communications Sector Disruption
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple A basic-skilled threat group leverages phishing to compromise communications infrastructure, executes payloads via PowerShell, and disrupts services through network flooding.

threat_actor_profile.campaign_or_group: name=RedDelta Modified PlugX Infection Chain Operations

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Domain Name Registrar, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, PowerShell Script, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends deceptive emails or messages to employees, exploiting human error to gain initial access to internet-facing assets. This technique leverages social engineering to trick recipients into clicking malicious links or attachments.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: After compromising credentials via phishing, the attacker deploys a PowerShell script through the domain name registrar's administrative interface. This allows execution of malicious code on targeted systems, establishing a foothold in the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker floods network traffic with malformed packets, overwhelming infrastructure and causing denial-of-service conditions. This disrupts communication services by preventing legitimate traffic from reaching its destination.

Scenario.summary: A basic-skilled threat group compromises communications infrastructure through phishing, executes payloads via PowerShell, and disrupts services by overwhelming network traffic with malicious data.
